program.name: Tiny Leela browser engine dovetail research
program.objective: Build a tiny in-browser Leela-like chess engine with policy/WDL neural evaluation, PUCT search, ONNX Runtime Web deployment, and verified chess correctness.

S0001.name: Phase C fixed teacher distillation consensus
S0001.reason: Migrate Tiny Leela from mixed bootstrap/Phase B/autoresearch logs to a dovetail-owned Phase C segment with fixed consensus-label student score.

L000.title: baseline trajectory
L000.hypothesis: Improve from the root baseline while scheduler spawns diverse lanes.

L001.title: True research lane: PUCT search policy experiments
L001.hypothesis: Compare structural root/search policy choices against fixed evaluator fixtures under benchmark tiny_leela_phase_b_fixed_metrics_v1.
L001.prune_reason: saturated: duplicate early search lane; keep evidence but route new work through richer benchmarks only

L002.title: True research lane: PUCT search policy experiments
L002.hypothesis: Once the substrate is stable, compare structural PUCT choices such as FPU, policy temperature, root noise, and uncertainty-based playout allocation against fixed evaluator fixtures.
L002.prune_reason: saturated: prior-transform search experiments exhausted under current policy-only fixtures; reopen only after value/search targets or benchmark v2

L003.title: True research lane: browser inference backends
L003.hypothesis: Compare ORT Web WASM/SIMD, WebGPU/FP16, worker batching, and model caching for latency and compatibility without changing chess semantics.
L003.prune_reason: saturated: runtime strategy best is progressive_fallback under estimated profiles; reopen after real model artifact and measured browser inference

L004.title: True research lane: tiny student architectures
L004.hypothesis: Compare micro and small conv student architecture specs under fixed Phase B policy/WDL metrics to find better strength-per-byte candidates.
L004.prune_reason: paused: architecture proxy lane is superseded by real teacher-distillation metrics; reopen only to evaluate trained/exported student variants

L005.title: Foundation/training-data: lc0 teacher distillation pipeline
L005.hypothesis: Make real lc0 distillation runnable by adding teacher availability checks, FEN seed export, teacher-label schema, and query script that fails clearly until lc0 binary and weights are configured.
L005.prune_reason: complete: lc0/Stockfish teacher setup and data pipeline are ready; do not schedule as research unless setup breaks

L006.title: True research lane: first real teacher-distilled student
L006.hypothesis: A tiny trainable student fit to real lc0/Stockfish teacher labels can produce measurable policy/WDL validation losses and replace architecture-only proxy metrics.

L007.title: True research lane: board-square student representation
L007.hypothesis: A student using explicit 64-square piece/color features should beat the current aggregate linear FEN baseline on fixed teacher_distill_seed_v2 policy/WDL metrics without changing labels.

L008.title: True research lane: small MLP student
L008.hypothesis: A dependency-free small hidden-layer student should learn non-linear board patterns and improve distillation score over linear features on teacher_distill_seed_v2.

L009.title: True research lane: teacher-source ablations
L009.hypothesis: Training on lc0-only, Stockfish-only, and combined teacher labels will identify whether the two teachers help or conflict on the fixed v2 seed dataset.

decision.reason: Initial baseline

N0001.hypothesis: Create minimal TypeScript/Node test scaffold so engine infrastructure can be built and verified in future lanes.

decision.reason: Bootstrap score rose from 0 to 32 and checks pass. The scheduler is currently treating foundational infrastructure as normal ideas, which is useful for tracking but can feel over-fragmented before the engine skeleton exists.

N0002.hypothesis: Convert the over-fragmented pre-work into an explicit L000 foundation substrate and separate true research-lane ideas from shared engine infrastructure.

decision.reason: The setup now has a minimum shared engine substrate and the idea queue distinguishes blocking pre-work from differentiated lanes. Bootstrap score improved from 32 to 98 with checks passing.

N0003.hypothesis: Clean benchmark/check scripts so Node's built-in test runner does not receive an invalid Vitest-style --run argument.

decision.reason: Metric remains 98, but validation output is cleaner and checks still pass. This is a stabilize-only cleanup, not a research lane.

N0004.hypothesis: Finish taxonomy cleanup by moving remaining spec/schema/gauntlet scaffold tasks out of the queued research-lane pool.

decision.reason: The idea queue now has 9 foundation milestones separated from 8 true research lanes. Checks pass with score 98.

N0005.hypothesis: Complete Phase A/B separation by documenting lab/product substrate vs frozen evaluation metrics and making Phase B metric readiness executable.

decision.reason: Phase A/B are now explicit: build substrate and evaluation infrastructure are pre-work, while Phase C research optimizes fixed metrics like policy_top1_acc, wdl_cross_entropy, fixed_playout_suite_score, latency, and model size. Readiness score improved to 130 and checks pass.

N0006.hypothesis: For the first search lane experiment, compare a structural root visit policy that preserves evaluator prior mass against the previous argmax-only root choice on frozen Phase B fixtures.

decision.reason: On the fixed 3-position fixture suite, argmax scored 33.849477 with CE 13.078683, while prior_proportional scored 73.468924 with CE 1.101706 and top1/top3 remained 1.0. This is an early toy-fixture result, not a chess-strength claim.

N0007.hypothesis: Compare a progressive WebGPU/WASM fallback runtime strategy against a WebGPU-only baseline under fixed browser capability profiles without changing chess semantics.

decision.reason: Progressive fallback scored 83.525 with 3/4 compatible profiles and 147.5ms median estimated latency; manual WebGPU-only baseline scored 44.575 with 2/4 compatible profiles and 542.5ms median. This is a fixed-profile runtime strategy result, not measured ORT latency yet.

N0008.hypothesis: A sqrt-prior root distribution may smooth overconfident teacher priors while preserving top move, potentially improving suite score versus prior-proportional.

decision.reason: sqrt_prior scored 71.610243 with CE 1.167327, worse than prior_proportional's 73.468924 and CE 1.101706 while preserving top1/top3. On current fixed fixtures, preserving full teacher prior mass is better than square-root smoothing.

N0009.hypothesis: Rerun prior_proportional in the search lane after sqrt_prior ablation to confirm the current local best under fixed fixtures before moving to a different structural idea.

decision.reason: Result exactly reproduced fixed_playout_suite_score=73.468924 and CE=1.101706. Current deterministic suite has no noise, so further simple prior transforms are unlikely to beat exact teacher-prior preservation.

N0010.hypothesis: Formally log the argmax root policy as the search-lane baseline to make the prior_proportional improvement auditable in the research tree.

decision.reason: Argmax preserves top1/top3 on the tiny suite but collapses the teacher distribution, scoring only 33.849477 with CE 13.078683 versus prior_proportional 73.468924/CE 1.101706. This confirms distribution-sensitive metrics are necessary; top-k alone cannot distinguish these policies.

N0011.hypothesis: Follow scheduler assignment in L002 by rerunning the only non-degenerate current-best root policy before forcing rotation away from exhausted policy-only search transforms.

decision.reason: Deterministic result again equals fixed_playout_suite_score=73.468924 and CE=1.101706. This confirms the policy-only search lane has saturated: because the benchmark rewards matching teacher distribution, exact prior preservation is the optimum among current transforms.

N0012.hypothesis: Under scheduler pressure to continue L002, re-evaluate sqrt_prior as a second lane-local check to confirm it is not competitive with exact prior preservation.

decision.reason: sqrt_prior again scores 71.610243 vs prior_proportional 73.468924. This duplicate result reinforces that L002 should be paused under current fixtures; more policy-only transform work is tunnel vision.

N0013.hypothesis: A simpler WebGPU-with-WASM-fallback runtime strategy might match progressive fallback compatibility while avoiding progressive/caching complexity.

decision.reason: wasm_fallback scored 73.525 with same 3/4 compatible profiles and 147.5ms median as progressive_fallback, but loses cache/progressive loading bonus. Progressive_fallback remains the current L003 best at 83.525.

N0014.hypothesis: Formally log WebGPU-only runtime strategy as the L003 baseline to make fallback benefits auditable.

decision.reason: WebGPU-only scores 44.575 with 2/4 compatible profiles and 542.5ms median penalty due unsupported browsers. This confirms browser runtime research must optimize compatibility and fallback, not only fastest backend.

N0015.hypothesis: Confirm progressive_fallback remains the deterministic L003 best after logging webgpu_only and wasm_fallback baselines.

decision.reason: Progressive_fallback exactly reproduced browser_compatibility_score=83.525, compatible_profiles=3/4, median=147.5. L003 is now saturated under estimated-profile metric until a real model artifact allows measured latency.

N0016.hypothesis: Compare tiny conv student architecture specs with a frozen capacity-vs-size proxy; practical 64x6 INT8 may offer the best policy-quality proxy while staying sub-1MB.

decision.reason: Manual sweep: 16x2=58.31, 24x3=65.18, 48x5=75.02, 64x6=78.35 architecture_score. 64x6 INT8 wins under this frozen proxy at 0.590MB and estimated policy_top1=0.585. This is architecture proxy research, not trained-model evidence.

N0017.hypothesis: Set up the real lc0 teacher distillation pipeline enough to detect whether actual distillation can run and to generate/validate teacher-label data once lc0 and weights are supplied.

decision.reason: The pipeline scaffolding is ready and tests pass, but this environment has no lc0 binary or weights configured: lc0_binary_ready=0, lc0_weights_ready=0, distillation_ready=0. Actual Leela distillation cannot run until LC0_BIN and LC0_WEIGHTS are provided.

N0018.hypothesis: Use current web references to make teacher setup actionable for lc0 and add Stockfish as an optional second teacher with separate labels.

decision.reason: Web search confirms lc0 source/binary and weights are separate: lc0 docs point to network pages for .pb.gz weights and to WeightsFile/--weights configuration. Stockfish has official downloads/UCI docs and Ubuntu package candidate, making it a practical fallback/second teacher. Neither binary is installed locally, so readiness metrics remain zero.

N0019.hypothesis: Install teacher engines locally without sudo so the distillation data pipeline can actually run.

decision.reason: Both teacher engines are now locally usable via env vars. lc0 is at .local_engines/lc0/build/release/lc0 with weights .local_engines/lc0_weights/weights_run2_912822.lc0. Stockfish is at .local_engines/stockfish_pkg/usr/games/stockfish. Smoke-generated 3 lc0 labels with nodes=1 and 3 Stockfish labels with depth=1; both validate.

N0020.hypothesis: A first dependency-free student trained on real lc0 and Stockfish labels can establish measurable distillation losses and create a baseline for future model/training research.

decision.reason: The full teacher-to-student loop now runs. Baseline is intentionally weak and tiny: 6 teacher rows, 20-move vocabulary, dev_policy_ce=3.121585, dev_wdl_ce=1.794682, dev_policy_top1=0.666667. Lc0 labels were one-hot under the current UCI parser at nodes=64, while Stockfish produced richer MultiPV distributions. This is still data-pipeline/model-quality evidence, not Elo.

N0021.hypothesis: Expanding teacher seed positions and extracting lc0 verbose root visit distributions will create a more meaningful distillation dataset than the initial 3-position/one-hot labels.

decision.reason: Dataset expanded from 6 to 20 teacher rows and move vocabulary from 20 to 44. Lc0 labels are no longer one-hot: avg_policy_moves=3.3; Stockfish avg_policy_moves=4.0. The linear student score fell from 16.90 on the tiny easy fixture to 12.05 on v2, with dev_policy_ce=6.57, showing the new dataset is harder and the linear FEN baseline is inadequate.

N0022.hypothesis: Explicit 64-square piece/color linear features should improve over aggregate FEN counts on fixed v2 teacher labels.

decision.reason: Board-square linear features improved train losses and slightly improved dev_policy_ce (6.44 vs 6.57), but worsened dev_wdl_ce enough that primary distill_student_score fell from 12.046814 to 11.555163. With only 20 rows and 770 features, the linear board-square model appears to overfit; richer features need regularization or non-linear sharing, not a wider linear one-hot board.

N0023.hypothesis: A dependency-free small hidden-layer student should improve over aggregate linear features on v2 teacher labels by modeling non-linear patterns.

decision.reason: MLP overfit/failed on the tiny v2 dataset: train losses improved versus linear but dev_policy_ce worsened to 7.595354 and dev_wdl_ce to 1.819077, dropping score from 12.046814 to 9.602060. Non-linear capacity is premature without more data or regularization.

N0024.hypothesis: Training on lc0-only, Stockfish-only, and combined teacher labels will reveal whether the two teachers help or conflict on fixed v2 labels.

decision.reason: Combined labels remain best on the primary score: combined=12.046814, Stockfish-only=11.723282, lc0-only=10.643424. Stockfish-only had better dev_top1=0.2 and lower dev_wdl_ce=0.612093, while combined had better dev_policy_ce=6.566377. lc0-only is weakest with current low-node labels.

N0025.hypothesis: Training-only draw regularization for the WDL head should reduce overfitting while preserving the fixed consensus-label evaluation target.

decision.reason: Draw regularization did not help the current head-separated model: score fell from 12.922135 to 12.890689. Policy CE was unchanged, but dev_wdl_ce regressed from 0.745483 to 0.764361, so the prior best WDL interactions should remain unchanged.

N0026.hypothesis: Segment baseline: Phase C fixed teacher distillation consensus

decision.reason: Migrate Tiny Leela from mixed bootstrap/Phase B/autoresearch logs to a dovetail-owned Phase C segment with fixed consensus-label student score.

N0027.hypothesis: Training the WDL head from Stockfish-only value targets while keeping consensus policy/evaluation should exploit Stockfish's lower WDL validation loss without sacrificing combined policy labels.

decision.reason: Stockfish-only WDL training hurt the consensus evaluation target: score fell from current best 12.922135 to 12.841531, with dev_wdl_ce worsening from 0.745483 to 0.794058 while policy CE stayed unchanged. The consensus WDL label is better aligned with the current benchmark than Stockfish-only WDL despite earlier raw-source ablation hints.

N0028.hypothesis: Polyak/SWA-style weight averaging over the second half of training should reduce tiny-dataset SGD overfit/variance while preserving the fixed consensus-label benchmark.

decision.reason: Weight averaging improved the fixed consensus benchmark from current best 12.922135 to 13.309426. The gain came from better dev_policy_ce (5.993176 -> 5.757296) while dev_wdl_ce slightly worsened (0.745483 -> 0.756176). Training losses rose, consistent with reduced overfit rather than stronger memorization.

N0029.hypothesis: A deterministic confirmation rerun of the averaged-weight student should reproduce the current best fixed consensus distillation score before further exploration.

decision.reason: The averaged-weight candidate exactly reproduced distill_student_score=13.309426, dev_policy_ce=5.757296, and dev_wdl_ce=0.756176 with checks passing. The current benchmark is deterministic for this candidate, so the improvement over 12.922135 is stable under rerun.

N0030.hypothesis: The baseline trajectory should adopt the current averaged-weight distillation benchmark result so L000 reflects the active Phase C segment best rather than the pre-averaging baseline.

decision.reason: L000 reproduces the current best distill_student_score=13.309426 with checks passing. This synchronizes the baseline trajectory with the kept L006 averaged-weight candidate; no new model behavior was introduced.

N0031.hypothesis: Adding explicit alternate-holdout fold reporting should improve robustness visibility without changing the fixed primary benchmark score or training target.

decision.reason: Primary distill_student_score stayed exactly 13.309426, preserving benchmark comparability. The two-fold mean is 12.742533 with std 0.566893, showing the tiny 10-position consensus split is sensitive to holdout choice; future improvements should be checked against fold robustness, not only the primary split.

R0001.hypothesis: Create minimal TypeScript/Node test scaffold so engine infrastructure can be built and verified in future lanes.
R0001.implementation: Added package.json, tsconfig.json, src/index.ts, tests/smoke.test.mjs, and made checks only run tsc when TypeScript is installed locally.
R0001.learned: Bootstrap score rose from 0 to 32 and checks pass. The scheduler is currently treating foundational infrastructure as normal ideas, which is useful for tracking but can feel over-fragmented before the engine skeleton exists.
R0001.next_action: Treat core infra as L000 foundation/backlog or a milestone checklist, then spawn separate lanes only after board/movegen/codec/search interfaces exist.

R0002.hypothesis: Convert the over-fragmented pre-work into an explicit L000 foundation substrate and separate true research-lane ideas from shared engine infrastructure.
R0002.implementation: Added board/FEN parsing, UCI/action move codec, pseudo-legal move generation and makeMove, feature encoder, evaluator interface, minimal search entry point, move encoding/browser docs, substrate tests, and reclassified loaded infra ideas as foundation_milestone while appending true research lanes.
R0002.learned: The setup now has a minimum shared engine substrate and the idea queue distinguishes blocking pre-work from differentiated lanes. Bootstrap score improved from 32 to 98 with checks passing.
R0002.next_action: Continue L000 only for hard correctness gaps such as full legal check/perft fixtures; otherwise schedule true lanes for search/runtime/model/training/evaluation comparisons.

R0003.hypothesis: Clean benchmark/check scripts so Node's built-in test runner does not receive an invalid Vitest-style --run argument.
R0003.implementation: Removed npm test -- --run fallback from dovetail.sh and dovetail.checks.sh; both now call npm test directly.
R0003.learned: Metric remains 98, but validation output is cleaner and checks still pass. This is a stabilize-only cleanup, not a research lane.
R0003.next_action: Commit cleanup and mark the goal complete after confirming clean git status.

R0004.hypothesis: Finish taxonomy cleanup by moving remaining spec/schema/gauntlet scaffold tasks out of the queued research-lane pool.
R0004.implementation: Reclassified I0009, I0011, and I0012 as foundation_milestone with low priority; queued high-priority ideas are now true differentiated lanes only.
R0004.learned: The idea queue now has 9 foundation milestones separated from 8 true research lanes. Checks pass with score 98.
R0004.next_action: Use schedule_next for actual lane work; if scheduler still picks L000, interpret it as foundation hardening only.

R0005.hypothesis: Complete Phase A/B separation by documenting lab/product substrate vs frozen evaluation metrics and making Phase B metric readiness executable.
R0005.implementation: Added docs/research_phases.md, eval/benchmark_spec.json, eval/phase_b_metrics.mjs, tests/phase_b.test.mjs, npm eval:phase-b script, and checks that validate Phase B metrics. Updated autoresearch.md and readiness scoring to state tiny_leela_score is lab-readiness only.
R0005.learned: Phase A/B are now explicit: build substrate and evaluation infrastructure are pre-work, while Phase C research optimizes fixed metrics like policy_top1_acc, wdl_cross_entropy, fixed_playout_suite_score, latency, and model size. Readiness score improved to 130 and checks pass.
R0005.next_action: Commit Phase A/B freeze, then optionally start a new Phase C benchmark segment when a real lane has an executable candidate metric.

R0006.hypothesis: For the first search lane experiment, compare a structural root visit policy that preserves evaluator prior mass against the previous argmax-only root choice on frozen Phase B fixtures.
R0006.implementation: Added eval/search_suite.mjs with fixed metrics for root distributions, src/search/rootPolicy.ts with argmax/prior_proportional/sqrt_prior variants, an npm eval:search script, and a regression test proving prior_proportional improves distribution score over argmax while preserving top-1.
R0006.learned: On the fixed 3-position fixture suite, argmax scored 33.849477 with CE 13.078683, while prior_proportional scored 73.468924 with CE 1.101706 and top1/top3 remained 1.0. This is an early toy-fixture result, not a chess-strength claim.
R0006.next_action: Ablate sqrt_prior or add value-weighted one-ply root policy, but do not change the fixed benchmark fixtures in this lane.

R0007.hypothesis: Compare a progressive WebGPU/WASM fallback runtime strategy against a WebGPU-only baseline under fixed browser capability profiles without changing chess semantics.
R0007.implementation: Added src/browser/backendPlan.ts with webgpu_only, wasm_fallback, and progressive_fallback strategies; added eval/runtime_suite.mjs and tests/runtime_suite.test.mjs to score compatibility, median estimated latency, caching, and progressive loading under fixed profiles.
R0007.learned: Progressive fallback scored 83.525 with 3/4 compatible profiles and 147.5ms median estimated latency; manual WebGPU-only baseline scored 44.575 with 2/4 compatible profiles and 542.5ms median. This is a fixed-profile runtime strategy result, not measured ORT latency yet.
R0007.next_action: Continue runtime lane by replacing estimated latencies with measured mock/evaluator latency or add explicit model-artifact prerequisite before ORT backend experiments.

R0008.hypothesis: A sqrt-prior root distribution may smooth overconfident teacher priors while preserving top move, potentially improving suite score versus prior-proportional.
R0008.implementation: No code change; evaluated existing sqrt_prior variant in eval/search_suite.mjs using TINY_LEELA_SEARCH_VARIANT=sqrt_prior.
R0008.learned: sqrt_prior scored 71.610243 with CE 1.167327, worse than prior_proportional's 73.468924 and CE 1.101706 while preserving top1/top3. On current fixed fixtures, preserving full teacher prior mass is better than square-root smoothing.
R0008.next_action: Do not pursue sqrt_prior on this fixture suite. Next search-lane candidate should add value-aware one-ply selection or uncertainty-based allocation rather than another monotonic prior transform.

R0009.hypothesis: Rerun prior_proportional in the search lane after sqrt_prior ablation to confirm the current local best under fixed fixtures before moving to a different structural idea.
R0009.implementation: No code change; evaluated existing prior_proportional variant in eval/search_suite.mjs.
R0009.learned: Result exactly reproduced fixed_playout_suite_score=73.468924 and CE=1.101706. Current deterministic suite has no noise, so further simple prior transforms are unlikely to beat exact teacher-prior preservation.
R0009.next_action: Search lane should either add new per-move value fixtures to a new benchmark version or pause until model/evaluator outputs create non-teacher priors; scheduler should rotate to architecture/training/evaluation lanes.

R0010.hypothesis: Formally log the argmax root policy as the search-lane baseline to make the prior_proportional improvement auditable in the research tree.
R0010.implementation: No code change; evaluated existing argmax variant in eval/search_suite.mjs.
R0010.learned: Argmax preserves top1/top3 on the tiny suite but collapses the teacher distribution, scoring only 33.849477 with CE 13.078683 versus prior_proportional 73.468924/CE 1.101706. This confirms distribution-sensitive metrics are necessary; top-k alone cannot distinguish these policies.
R0010.next_action: Search lane has exhausted current fixed-policy-only variants; rotate away or create a new benchmark version with per-move value/search targets before further search work.

R0011.hypothesis: Follow scheduler assignment in L002 by rerunning the only non-degenerate current-best root policy before forcing rotation away from exhausted policy-only search transforms.
R0011.implementation: No code change; evaluated prior_proportional search suite under frozen fixtures.
R0011.learned: Deterministic result again equals fixed_playout_suite_score=73.468924 and CE=1.101706. This confirms the policy-only search lane has saturated: because the benchmark rewards matching teacher distribution, exact prior preservation is the optimum among current transforms.
R0011.next_action: Scheduler should rotate to a non-search lane or the program should add a new benchmark version with per-move values before additional search experiments.

R0012.hypothesis: Under scheduler pressure to continue L002, re-evaluate sqrt_prior as a second lane-local check to confirm it is not competitive with exact prior preservation.
R0012.implementation: No code change; reran existing sqrt_prior variant.
R0012.learned: sqrt_prior again scores 71.610243 vs prior_proportional 73.468924. This duplicate result reinforces that L002 should be paused under current fixtures; more policy-only transform work is tunnel vision.
R0012.next_action: Force rotation by creating/scheduling architecture or training lane; do not spend further L002 budget until benchmark evolves.

R0013.hypothesis: A simpler WebGPU-with-WASM-fallback runtime strategy might match progressive fallback compatibility while avoiding progressive/caching complexity.
R0013.implementation: No code change; evaluated existing wasm_fallback runtime strategy under fixed browser profiles.
R0013.learned: wasm_fallback scored 73.525 with same 3/4 compatible profiles and 147.5ms median as progressive_fallback, but loses cache/progressive loading bonus. Progressive_fallback remains the current L003 best at 83.525.
R0013.next_action: Do not pursue plain wasm_fallback unless complexity budget becomes a primary metric; runtime lane should next require measured latency or model artifact.

R0014.hypothesis: Formally log WebGPU-only runtime strategy as the L003 baseline to make fallback benefits auditable.
R0014.implementation: No code change; evaluated existing webgpu_only strategy under fixed browser profiles.
R0014.learned: WebGPU-only scores 44.575 with 2/4 compatible profiles and 542.5ms median penalty due unsupported browsers. This confirms browser runtime research must optimize compatibility and fallback, not only fastest backend.
R0014.next_action: Pause L003 until measured model/evaluator latency exists; rotate to architecture/training lane.

R0015.hypothesis: Confirm progressive_fallback remains the deterministic L003 best after logging webgpu_only and wasm_fallback baselines.
R0015.implementation: No code change; reran existing progressive_fallback strategy under fixed browser profiles.
R0015.learned: Progressive_fallback exactly reproduced browser_compatibility_score=83.525, compatible_profiles=3/4, median=147.5. L003 is now saturated under estimated-profile metric until a real model artifact allows measured latency.
R0015.next_action: Rotate to L004 architecture or training lane; avoid more runtime strategy repeats.

R0016.hypothesis: Compare tiny conv student architecture specs with a frozen capacity-vs-size proxy; practical 64x6 INT8 may offer the best policy-quality proxy while staying sub-1MB.
R0016.implementation: Added src/nn/modelSpec.ts for deterministic parameter/size/policy estimates, eval/architecture_suite.mjs, npm eval:architecture, and tests/architecture_suite.test.mjs. Evaluated practical_64x6_int8 as the best observed candidate among manual micro/small/balanced/practical comparisons.
R0016.learned: Manual sweep: 16x2=58.31, 24x3=65.18, 48x5=75.02, 64x6=78.35 architecture_score. 64x6 INT8 wins under this frozen proxy at 0.590MB and estimated policy_top1=0.585. This is architecture proxy research, not trained-model evidence.
R0016.next_action: Ablate balanced_48x5 vs practical_64x6 tradeoff, or create training lane to replace proxy estimates with teacher-label validation.

R0017.hypothesis: Set up the real lc0 teacher distillation pipeline enough to detect whether actual distillation can run and to generate/validate teacher-label data once lc0 and weights are supplied.
R0017.implementation: Added scripts/check_lc0_teacher.py, scripts/export_seed_fens.py, scripts/query_lc0_teacher.py, training/teacher_schema.md, training/validate_teacher_labels.py, data/seed_positions.fen, data/teacher_labels.sample.jsonl, npm distill:* scripts, and tests/distillation_setup.test.mjs.
R0017.learned: The pipeline scaffolding is ready and tests pass, but this environment has no lc0 binary or weights configured: lc0_binary_ready=0, lc0_weights_ready=0, distillation_ready=0. Actual Leela distillation cannot run until LC0_BIN and LC0_WEIGHTS are provided.
R0017.next_action: Install/provide lc0 and a teacher weights file, then run `npm run distill:seed`, `LC0_BIN=... LC0_WEIGHTS=... npm run distill:query`, and `npm run distill:validate -- data/teacher_labels.jsonl`.

R0018.hypothesis: Use current web references to make teacher setup actionable for lc0 and add Stockfish as an optional second teacher with separate labels.
R0018.implementation: Added docs/teacher_setup.md with current lc0/Stockfish sources and commands. Added Stockfish readiness/query scripts and npm stockfish:* commands. Tests now cover both teacher checkers without requiring engines in CI.
R0018.learned: Web search confirms lc0 source/binary and weights are separate: lc0 docs point to network pages for .pb.gz weights and to WeightsFile/--weights configuration. Stockfish has official downloads/UCI docs and Ubuntu package candidate, making it a practical fallback/second teacher. Neither binary is installed locally, so readiness metrics remain zero.
R0018.next_action: Install lc0 plus network weights and/or Stockfish, set LC0_BIN/LC0_WEIGHTS or STOCKFISH_BIN, then generate real labels.

R0019.hypothesis: Install teacher engines locally without sudo so the distillation data pipeline can actually run.
R0019.implementation: Installed Stockfish locally by downloading/extracting Ubuntu stockfish package into .local_engines. Built lc0 v0.32.1 locally from source with Meson/Ninja in a venv, downloaded latest training network weights from training.lczero.org, fixed lc0 readiness checker to use UCI instead of unsupported --version, and ignored local engine artifacts/real generated labels.
R0019.learned: Both teacher engines are now locally usable via env vars. lc0 is at .local_engines/lc0/build/release/lc0 with weights .local_engines/lc0_weights/weights_run2_912822.lc0. Stockfish is at .local_engines/stockfish_pkg/usr/games/stockfish. Smoke-generated 3 lc0 labels with nodes=1 and 3 Stockfish labels with depth=1; both validate.
R0019.next_action: Run higher-quality label generation with more lc0 nodes / Stockfish depth, then start a real student training script against generated JSONL labels.

R0020.hypothesis: A first dependency-free student trained on real lc0 and Stockfish labels can establish measurable distillation losses and create a baseline for future model/training research.
R0020.implementation: Generated stronger real teacher labels for 3 seed positions using lc0 nodes=64/multipv=4 and Stockfish depth=10/multipv=4. Added training/train_student.py, a pure-Python linear FEN-feature student with policy and WDL heads, npm distill:train, and tests/train_student.test.mjs.
R0020.learned: The full teacher-to-student loop now runs. Baseline is intentionally weak and tiny: 6 teacher rows, 20-move vocabulary, dev_policy_ce=3.121585, dev_wdl_ce=1.794682, dev_policy_top1=0.666667. Lc0 labels were one-hot under the current UCI parser at nodes=64, while Stockfish produced richer MultiPV distributions. This is still data-pipeline/model-quality evidence, not Elo.
R0020.next_action: Improve teacher label extraction quality (lc0 visit-policy/WDL if exposed, more seed positions), then compare student families or add a small neural/ONNX training backend.

R0021.hypothesis: Expanding teacher seed positions and extracting lc0 verbose root visit distributions will create a more meaningful distillation dataset than the initial 3-position/one-hot labels.
R0021.implementation: Added data/distill_seed_positions.fen with 10 curated opening/middlegame/endgame positions. Updated lc0 query parsing to prefer VerboseMoveStats root visits/WL/D and to overwrite final MultiPV lines instead of freezing early shallow PVs. Updated Stockfish parser similarly. Regenerated lc0 nodes=16 and Stockfish depth=8 labels for 10 positions and trained the linear baseline.
R0021.learned: Dataset expanded from 6 to 20 teacher rows and move vocabulary from 20 to 44. Lc0 labels are no longer one-hot: avg_policy_moves=3.3; Stockfish avg_policy_moves=4.0. The linear student score fell from 16.90 on the tiny easy fixture to 12.05 on v2, with dev_policy_ce=6.57, showing the new dataset is harder and the linear FEN baseline is inadequate.
R0021.next_action: Compare stronger student representations on v2: board-square features or a small MLP/conv implementation. Also consider more lc0 nodes now that verbose visit extraction works.

R0022.hypothesis: Explicit 64-square piece/color linear features should improve over aggregate FEN counts on fixed v2 teacher labels.
R0022.implementation: Added optional board_square feature extractor to the pure-Python linear student and tested it against the same 20-row lc0+Stockfish v2 labels.
R0022.learned: Board-square linear features improved train losses and slightly improved dev_policy_ce (6.44 vs 6.57), but worsened dev_wdl_ce enough that primary distill_student_score fell from 12.046814 to 11.555163. With only 20 rows and 770 features, the linear board-square model appears to overfit; richer features need regularization or non-linear sharing, not a wider linear one-hot board.
R0022.next_action: Discard as primary regression. Try a small MLP/shared representation or source/loss ablations rather than high-dimensional linear square features.

R0023.hypothesis: A dependency-free small hidden-layer student should improve over aggregate linear features on v2 teacher labels by modeling non-linear patterns.
R0023.implementation: Added optional tanh MLP student with 16 hidden units to training/train_student.py and evaluated it on the fixed 20-row lc0+Stockfish v2 labels.
R0023.learned: MLP overfit/failed on the tiny v2 dataset: train losses improved versus linear but dev_policy_ce worsened to 7.595354 and dev_wdl_ce to 1.819077, dropping score from 12.046814 to 9.602060. Non-linear capacity is premature without more data or regularization.
R0023.next_action: Discard MLP candidate. Prefer teacher-source/loss ablations on the current linear baseline, or expand the dataset before adding capacity.

R0024.hypothesis: Training on lc0-only, Stockfish-only, and combined teacher labels will reveal whether the two teachers help or conflict on fixed v2 labels.
R0024.implementation: Ran source ablation using existing linear aggregate student: lc0-only, Stockfish-only, and combined lc0+Stockfish labels. No code changes were required.
R0024.learned: Combined labels remain best on the primary score: combined=12.046814, Stockfish-only=11.723282, lc0-only=10.643424. Stockfish-only had better dev_top1=0.2 and lower dev_wdl_ce=0.612093, while combined had better dev_policy_ce=6.566377. lc0-only is weakest with current low-node labels.
R0024.next_action: Keep combined teacher data as default. If optimizing top1/WDL separately, Stockfish-only is interesting; otherwise run policy/WDL loss-mix ablations next.

R0025.hypothesis: Training-only draw regularization for the WDL head should reduce overfitting while preserving the fixed consensus-label evaluation target.
R0025.implementation: Added train_wdl_target() with a fixed draw_regularized mode that shifts 10% WDL training mass to draw, and ran the fixed distillation benchmark through the internal dovetail runner.
R0025.learned: Draw regularization did not help the current head-separated model: score fell from 12.922135 to 12.890689. Policy CE was unchanged, but dev_wdl_ce regressed from 0.745483 to 0.764361, so the prior best WDL interactions should remain unchanged.
R0025.next_action: Discard and revert. Avoid WDL regularization on this tiny fixed seed unless validated across a broader cross-fold or larger label set.

R0026.hypothesis: Training the WDL head from Stockfish-only value targets while keeping consensus policy/evaluation should exploit Stockfish's lower WDL validation loss without sacrificing combined policy labels.
R0026.implementation: Added source-aware merged rows that retain per-teacher WDL labels and a --wdl-train-source option; ran the fixed dovetail distillation benchmark with --wdl-train-source stockfish.
R0026.learned: Stockfish-only WDL training hurt the consensus evaluation target: score fell from current best 12.922135 to 12.841531, with dev_wdl_ce worsening from 0.745483 to 0.794058 while policy CE stayed unchanged. The consensus WDL label is better aligned with the current benchmark than Stockfish-only WDL despite earlier raw-source ablation hints.
R0026.next_action: Discard and revert. Do not specialize WDL to Stockfish under the consensus benchmark; next try robustness/cross-fold reporting or increase teacher-label coverage in a new segment.

R0027.hypothesis: Polyak/SWA-style weight averaging over the second half of training should reduce tiny-dataset SGD overfit/variance while preserving the fixed consensus-label benchmark.
R0027.implementation: Added --average-weights to the dependency-free student trainer. During the second half of training it accumulates policy and WDL weights each epoch, evaluates/serializes their average, and the fixed dovetail benchmark now enables this option.
R0027.learned: Weight averaging improved the fixed consensus benchmark from current best 12.922135 to 13.309426. The gain came from better dev_policy_ce (5.993176 -> 5.757296) while dev_wdl_ce slightly worsened (0.745483 -> 0.756176). Training losses rose, consistent with reduced overfit rather than stronger memorization.
R0027.next_action: Keep averaged weights as the new baseline. Confirm determinism once, then try robustness/cross-fold reporting or mild objective regularization only if it improves validation without adding feature dimensions.

R0028.hypothesis: A deterministic confirmation rerun of the averaged-weight student should reproduce the current best fixed consensus distillation score before further exploration.
R0028.implementation: No code changes. Reran the current dovetail benchmark ./dovetail.sh at commit c300245 with --average-weights enabled.
R0028.learned: The averaged-weight candidate exactly reproduced distill_student_score=13.309426, dev_policy_ce=5.757296, and dev_wdl_ce=0.756176 with checks passing. The current benchmark is deterministic for this candidate, so the improvement over 12.922135 is stable under rerun.
R0028.next_action: Continue automated dovetail exploration. Prefer robustness/cross-fold reporting or data-coverage improvements over adding more WDL feature dimensions or teacher-source specialization.

R0029.hypothesis: The baseline trajectory should adopt the current averaged-weight distillation benchmark result so L000 reflects the active Phase C segment best rather than the pre-averaging baseline.
R0029.implementation: No code changes. Ran the scheduler-assigned L000 quantum against the current fixed dovetail benchmark ./dovetail.sh, which includes consensus labels, WDL head interactions, and averaged weights.
R0029.learned: L000 reproduces the current best distill_student_score=13.309426 with checks passing. This synchronizes the baseline trajectory with the kept L006 averaged-weight candidate; no new model behavior was introduced.
R0029.next_action: Scheduler should return to L006 or spawn a new data/robustness lane for non-duplicate improvements such as cross-fold reporting or larger teacher-label coverage.

R0030.hypothesis: Adding explicit alternate-holdout fold reporting should improve robustness visibility without changing the fixed primary benchmark score or training target.
R0030.implementation: Refactored training into train_once() and added --report-folds. The primary metric remains the original holdout offset 0 score; when reporting is enabled the trainer also trains alternate holdout offsets and emits fold_score_mean/std as secondary diagnostics. dovetail.sh enables the reporting flag.
R0030.learned: Primary distill_student_score stayed exactly 13.309426, preserving benchmark comparability. The two-fold mean is 12.742533 with std 0.566893, showing the tiny 10-position consensus split is sensitive to holdout choice; future improvements should be checked against fold robustness, not only the primary split.
R0030.next_action: Keep robustness reporting as diagnostics. Next structural work should try to improve fold_score_mean or add more teacher-label coverage in a new segment, while leaving the fixed primary metric intact.
